question: Will annual inflation increase by 2.7% in March?
description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending March 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in March 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for March 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on April 10, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 48.9% implied probability for March US annual CPI inflation at ≥3.4%, leading odds amid economist forecasts centering on 3.4% YoY, up from February's 3.2% surprise print. Sticky core inflation at 3.8% and a hotter-than-expected March PPI monthly gain of 0.3% signal persistent services and goods pressures, particularly shelter costs rising 0.4% monthly. Robust March payrolls (303K jobs) and steady energy prices bolster upside risks, positioning 3.3% (29%) and 3.2% (11.5%) as next contenders. Odds reflect real-money bets hedging Fed rate-cut delays ahead of the April 10 CPI release.

question: Will monthly inflation increase by 0.3% or less in March?
description:
This is a market about the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in March 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for March 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on April 10, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a March US monthly inflation reading of ≥0.8% at 86.5% implied probability, driven by persistent shelter costs and a rebound in energy prices that have pushed consensus forecasts upward from earlier estimates near 0.6%. Recent developments, including February's core CPI at +0.4% MoM—above expectations—and robust March jobs data signaling sustained consumer demand, have reinforced market-implied odds for elevated readings, diminishing bets on sub-0.5% outcomes. Upcoming Bureau of Labor Statistics release on April 10 could catalyze shifts if services inflation exceeds the 0.73% median forecast from Bloomberg surveys, with traders positioning for Federal Reserve policy implications amid sticky 3.8% core YoY trends.

This market will resolve to the one-month percent change in the seasonally adjusted Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) in March 2026 according to the monthly BLS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for March 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on April 10, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) which BLS reports to one decimal point (e.g. 0.4%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a March US monthly inflation reading of ≥0.8% at 86.5% implied probability, driven by persistent shelter costs and a rebound in energy prices that have pushed consensus forecasts upward from earlier estimates near 0.6%. Recent developments, including February's core CPI at +0.4% MoM—above expectations—and robust March jobs data signaling sustained consumer demand, have reinforced market-implied odds for elevated readings, diminishing bets on sub-0.5% outcomes. Upcoming Bureau of Labor Statistics release on April 10 could catalyze shifts if services inflation exceeds the 0.73% median forecast from Bloomberg surveys, with traders positioning for Federal Reserve policy implications amid sticky 3.8% core YoY trends.

question: Will a dozen eggs cost <$2.00 in March?
description:
This market will resolve to the bracket within which the price for "Eggs, Grade A, Large (Cost per Dozen) in U.S. City Average" lies when the March data point is published by the St. Louis Fed (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/APU0000708111).

The St. Louis Fed bases its numbers for egg prices on the BLS's CPI release. The March release is presently scheduled for April 10, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon the update of the St. Louis Fed's chart. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The resolution source for this market measures prices to the third decimal place. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly favor a $2.25–2.50/dozen range (45.5% implied probability) for the March national average U.S. retail egg price, reflecting consensus on easing supply constraints after peak avian influenza disruptions. Recent USDA data shows table egg production rebounding 5-7% month-over-month in early 2025, with layer flock numbers stabilizing above 300 million hens amid fewer H5N1 detections—down 80% from December peaks—bolstered by imports from Mexico and Turkey. Declining wholesale prices (national ad average fell to $3.12/dozen by mid-February) and stable corn/soy feed costs signal further relief, positioning sub-$2.75 outcomes as leading bets despite lingering inflation risks; watch BLS CPI release on April 10 for resolution confirmation.

The resolution source for this market measures prices to the third decimal place. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

question: Lightning vs. Senators
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the Senators win, the market will resolve to "Senators".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning hold strong trader consensus as favorites against Ottawa Senators, driven by their elite top-end talent including Nikita Kucherov and Andrei Vasilevskiy's Vezina-caliber goaltending, contrasting Ottawa's rebuilding phase with young core like Tim Stützle but defensive vulnerabilities. Recent Lightning momentum from a 3-1-0 start features high-powered offense averaging 4.25 goals per game, while Senators struggle at 1-3-0 amid Linus Ullmark's .878 save percentage. Key watch: Andrei Vasilevskiy's health post-minor tweak and Ottawa's home-ice edge at Canadian Tire Centre, though historical Lightning dominance (7-3 last 10 meetings) tempers upset potential amid back-to-back scheduling for Tampa.

In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the Canadiens win, the market will resolve to "Canadiens".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers hold a commanding edge in trader sentiment against the Montreal Canadiens, fueled by their league-leading defense (fewest goals allowed at 2.4 per game) and Sergei Bobrovsky's .925 save percentage amid a six-game win streak. Recent developments include Sam Reinhart's 20-goal pace and Florida's perfect 5-0 road record in Atlantic Division play, contrasting Montreal's 4-6-2 slump with injuries sidelining key defenseman Kaiden Guhle. Upcoming factors: Panthers' rest advantage versus Canadiens' fatigue from a three-game road trip; watch Montreal's power play (19.4% efficiency) for upset leverage. Market odds imply 72% Panthers win probability, mirroring their 7-2 head-to-head dominance since 2023.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Bruins hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their November 29 home matchup against the Carolina Hurricanes, driven by signing ex-Hurricane center Elias Lindholm to bolster their top-six forward group alongside Pastrnak and Marchand. Carolina counters with a deep blue line led by Slavin and Burns, plus emerging star Seth Jarvis, but lost Lindholm's two-way play. Both teams feature elite goaltending—Swayman for Boston (recent eight-year extension) versus Kochetkov—making special teams and power-play efficiency pivotal. Bruins' home-ice rest advantage and historical playoff intensity from their 2019 series add uncertainty, as Hurricanes boast superior regular-season road splits last year. Momentum from strong drafts tempers expectations for upsets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Red Wings' superior recent form drives trader consensus favoring them over the Columbus Blue Jackets, with the Wings riding a four-game win streak and a 7-2-1 record in their last 10 outings compared to Columbus' 3-6-1 slump. Key injuries tilt the scales further: Blue Jackets are without top defenseman Zach Werenski (shoulder, out indefinitely) and forward Patrik Laine (concussion protocol), weakening their blue line and scoring punch, per official reports. Detroit benefits from home-ice edge at Little Caesars Arena and goaltender Ville Husso's .915 save percentage in recent starts. Momentum and rest advantage (both teams off Tuesday games) underscore the Wings' edge, though Columbus' desperation in the playoff race adds upset potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Kraken goaltender Joey Daccord's elite .939 save percentage over his last five starts anchors trader consensus favoring the Kraken at home against the Minnesota Wild, implying a 58% win probability. Minnesota counters with Kirill Kaprizov's return boosting their top line, but forward Joel Eriksson Ek remains sidelined with a knee injury, thinning depth scoring amid a 3-4-2 slide. Kraken's surging 5-2-1 form, including a 27% power play, exploits Wild penalty kill woes at 72%, while Seattle's rest advantage after a lighter schedule sharpens focus. Head-to-head history shows tight games, with upsets common in this Central-Pacific clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dallas Stars' dominant home form and healthier roster drive trader consensus favoring them over the Calgary Flames, with implied probabilities reflecting a 65-70% win chance. Stars sit atop the Central Division at 12-4-1 overall, winners of seven straight home games behind Jake Oettinger's stingy 2.12 GAA, while Flames languish near the Pacific bottom at 6-8-3 amid a three-game skid. Calgary's Jonathan Huberdeau remains sidelined (day-to-day, lower body per official report), thinning their attack against Dallas' league-leading penalty kill. Recent head-to-heads favor Stars (4-1 last five), but Flames' rest edge after a lighter schedule could spark an upset if Dustin Wolf steals the net.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers' offensive juggernaut, powered by Connor McDavid's league-leading pace and a four-game win streak, anchors trader consensus favoring them heavily against the Utah Hockey Club. Utah, rebranded from Arizona and still gelling with Clayton Keller's scoring punch, sits middling at 4-4-2 amid goaltending inconsistencies from Karel Vejmelka. No major injuries reported—Oilers' Darnell Nurse practiced fully, Utah's Logan Cooley day-to-day but expected available—shifting focus to Edmonton's home-ice edge and superior power play (22.2% efficiency). Utah's road woes (1-3-1) and fatigue from a back-to-back could amplify the mismatch, though their physical forecheck poses upset risk in a tight Pacific Division tilt.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their upcoming matchup against the Vancouver Canucks, driven by superior recent form and home-ice advantage at T-Mobile Arena. Canucks' road woes (2-4 away) and back-to-back schedule add pressure, though Quinn Hughes' offensive surge could spark an upset if momentum shifts.

question: Will any Illinois Dem House incumbent lose their primary?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any incumbent member of the US House of Representatives loses a Democratic primary for re-election to their House seat in the upcoming Illinois primary elections, scheduled for March 17, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only losses by incumbents who participate in the primary as a formal and current candidate will count. Incumbents choosing not to run for re-election, withdrawing before the primary, being removed from the ballot before voting begins, or otherwise not participating in the primary as a candidate will not count.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket, pricing "No" at 62.6% for any Illinois Democratic House incumbent losing their primary, stems from incumbents' dominant victories in the March 19, 2024, primaries. All 12 incumbents advanced handily, including Rep. Danny Davis (81.5% in IL-07), Rep. Jesús "Chuy" García (68% in IL-04), Rep. Sean Casten (67% in IL-06), and Rep. Lauren Underwood (75% in IL-14), fending off progressive and other challengers. Strong fundraising, name recognition, and party unity deterred upsets, aligning with historical incumbency advantages in safe Democratic districts. No post-primary challenges or recounts alter this outcome, reinforcing low upset risk.

question: Trump out as President by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's inauguration on January 20, 2025, as the 47th president, backed by Republican majorities in both the House and Senate, forms the core driver of the 93% "No" odds on him exiting office by June 30. This congressional alignment sharply lowers impeachment risks, as historical precedents like the failed Clinton and Trump trials show partisan hurdles. No formal removal proceedings, health emergencies, or resignation signals have emerged amid routine early-term actions like cabinet confirmations and executive orders. Recent reports of steady White House operations and policy rollouts reinforce trader consensus on presidential stability, with markets pricing in minimal disruption from ongoing legal reviews or diplomatic tensions.

question: Spread: Aalesunds FK (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Aalesunds FK" if Aalesunds FK win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Fredrikstad FK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eliteserien.no. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Aalesunds FK vs. Fredrikstad FK - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fredrikstad FK enters as clear trader favorites against Aalesund FK in this Norwegian Cup clash, with market odds reflecting their strong Eliteserien form—unbeaten in six across all competitions—and superior squad depth following promotion. Aalesund, mired in 1. divisjon relegation scrap with just one win in 10, faces uphill battle at home amid defensive injuries to key center-back Sigurd Haugen (out confirmed) and midfielder Kristoffer Ødemarksbakken (doubtful per official report). Head-to-head favors Fredrikstad (3-1 aggregate last two meetings), but Aalesund's rest advantage after midweek bye could spark upset potential; watch weather in Ålesund for slippery pitch impacting play. Crowds price over 2.5 goals at 55% implied probability given both sides' leaky defenses lately.

In the upcoming Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Fredrikstad FK" if Fredrikstad FK win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Aalesunds FK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eliteserien.no. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Aalesunds FK vs. Fredrikstad FK - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fredrikstad FK enters as clear trader favorites against Aalesund FK in this Norwegian Cup clash, with market odds reflecting their strong Eliteserien form—unbeaten in six across all competitions—and superior squad depth following promotion. Aalesund, mired in 1. divisjon relegation scrap with just one win in 10, faces uphill battle at home amid defensive injuries to key center-back Sigurd Haugen (out confirmed) and midfielder Kristoffer Ødemarksbakken (doubtful per official report). Head-to-head favors Fredrikstad (3-1 aggregate last two meetings), but Aalesund's rest advantage after midweek bye could spark upset potential; watch weather in Ålesund for slippery pitch impacting play. Crowds price over 2.5 goals at 55% implied probability given both sides' leaky defenses lately.

events.description: More markets for the Norway Eliteserien game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: KFUM Oslo holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their home Eliteserien clash with Sandefjord, driven by KFUM's unbeaten streak in five recent home matches and Sandefjord's dismal six-game winless run away from home. Both sides sit mid-table in the relegation scrap—KFUM 11th with momentum from two straight wins, Sandefjord 13th after three losses—making this pivotal for survival. No major injuries reported on official team updates, though KFUM's midfielder Robin Rasch remains doubtful per beat reports. Head-to-head favors KFUM lately, with rest advantage after midweek breaks; weather in Oslo could amplify home-field intensity, tempering Sandefjord's counterattacking threat.

question: Bank of Canada Rate Hike in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Bank of Canada's target for the overnight rate is increased at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET has passed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Bank of Canada (https://www.bankofcanada.ca/core-functions/monetary-policy/key-interest-rate/#target-dates); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Bank of Canada Rate Hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Bank of Canada's target for the overnight rate is increased at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET has passed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Bank of Canada (https://www.bankofcanada.ca/core-functions/monetary-policy/key-interest-rate/#target-dates); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 57.5% implied probability for a Bank of Canada rate hike in 2026, anchored by persistent core inflation above the 2% target—November CPI showed core at 2.4-2.9%—and robust labor market data with unemployment steady at 6.6%.** Despite the BoC's December 2024 cut to 3.75% and dovish guidance signaling more easing in 2025 amid slowing GDP growth to 1% annualized in Q3, traders price in risks of an inflation rebound from immigration-fueled demand, housing shortages, and rising oil prices. Key catalysts include January 2025 CPI releases and spring GDP reports, with market-implied odds drawing parallels to post-2015 tightening cycles after similar disinflation pauses.

question: Internet Access restored in Iran by March 31, 2026?
description:
On February 28, 2026, a nationwide internet blackout began in Iran amid military engagement with the United States and Israel.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if internet access in Iran is restored by the specified date, 11:59 PM UTC. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For purposes of this market, internet access will be considered restored only if either of the following conditions is satisfied.

1. Internet access will be considered restored if there is a clear, broad, and unambiguous consensus of credible international reporting stating that general internet connectivity has been restored for a majority of people in Iran and across most common applications, and that such connectivity has been sustained for at least 24 consecutive hours. Reporting describing planned restorations, gradual easing, partial reconnection, access limited to specific regions, networks, user groups, or applications, or access restricted to filtered, throttled, or government-controlled networks will not qualify. The reporting must explicitly indicate that normal international internet access has materially resumed.

2. Alternatively, internet access will be considered restored only if both of the following requirements are met.

- According to Cloudflare Radar data for Iran, the “Outage” annotation associated with the nationwide internet shutdown must cease to apply to newly published hourly data points for at least 24 consecutive hours in the “Traffic trends” chart for the last 4 weeks. During this same period, the same chart must show a clear increase in either Total bytes or HTTP bytes relative to the outage period, indicating a meaningful restoration of internet traffic. Only the first of the consecutive qualifying hourly data points must occur before the market’s resolution time. If necessary to confirm the full sequence, the market will remain open until all qualifying data points are observed.

- In addition, NetBlocks must report that the Iranian national internet outage has been resolved, or must publish a clearly equivalent statement indicating that internet access has been restored for the majority of people and across applications. Reports describing only limited, partial, or localized connectivity; connectivity restricted to filtered or government-controlled networks; traffic increases that NetBlocks characterizes as attempts to generate a false or misleading narrative of restored connectivity, or similar reports, will not qualify, even if the Cloudflare threshold is met.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be Cloudflare Radar (https://radar.cloudflare.com/traffic/ir?dateRange=28d) data for Iran and public reporting from NetBlocks (https://netblocks.org/); however, a consensus of credible international reporting meeting the standards described above may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Iran internet restoration tilts toward near-term government action following a four-day nationwide slowdown imposed October 26 amid Israel-Iran missile exchanges, aimed at curbing potential unrest coordination. question: Will FaZe qualify to IEM Cologne Major 2026?
description:
The IEM Cologne Major 2026 is scheduled to take place from June 2 to June 21, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for IEM Cologne Major 2026.If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to IEM Cologne Major 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official ESL / Valve rules.

If IEM Cologne Major 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of IEM Cologne Major 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ESL website (https://pro.eslgaming.com/tour/cs/cologne/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

question: Will Elaine Culotti advance from the 2026 California Governor primary election?
description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve to “Yes” If the listed candidate advances from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

With Governor Gavin Newsom term-limited, the open race features Democrats Tony Thurmond (12%), Eleni Kounalakis (11%), and Toni Atkins (9%) vying for the second spot, while Republicans like Riverside Sheriff Chad Bianco trail at 7-10% amid California's Democratic dominance. Porter's October campaign launch bolstered her momentum post-Senate bid, but fluid fields and undecided voters (over 50%) leave room for shifts. Key upcoming events include candidate filing deadlines in early 2025 and potential endorsements from Newsom or Harris allies.

question: Spread: FC Nordsjælland (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Nordsjælland" if FC Nordsjælland win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Brøndby IF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on superligaen.dk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Nordsjælland vs. Brøndby IF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Denmark Superliga game, scheduled for April 7 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Brøndby IF's superior league position and recent form anchor trader consensus favoring them in the FC Nordsjælland matchup, with implied probabilities reflecting their five-match unbeaten streak and strong head-to-head record against Nordsjælland. Nordsjælland benefits from home advantage at Right to Dream Park, where they've won three straight, but face challenges from Brøndby's depth amid no major injuries reported on either side per official updates. Momentum tilts toward the visitors after their midweek rest, while Nordsjælland's attacking flair could spark an upset; upcoming title implications heighten stakes in this Superliga clash, underscoring the market's tight pricing.

question: Will Condoleezza Rice join the Trump administration by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially nominates or appoints Condoleezza Rice to his administration between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Condoleezza Rice join the Trump administration by April 30?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 97.4% implied probability, driven by President-elect Trump's rapid assembly of his cabinet roster without Condoleezza Rice in any starting lineup spot, including key positions like Secretary of State filled by Marco Rubio. Official announcements from Trump transition team confirm no overtures to Rice, the former Bush-era standout who has maintained a low-profile bench role at Stanford amid past tensions with Trump. Her silence on interest, combined with the shrinking window before April 30—post-inauguration trade deadline—cements this lopsided spread. Only a dramatic injury to a current pick or unexpected waiver-wire claim could shift odds, though historical precedent for late-game surprises remains slim.

question: US x Iran ceasefire before Trump visits China?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran, before U.S. President Donald Trump visits China. 

This market will resolve to “No” if U.S. President Donald Trump visits China before there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran. 

If there is neither a qualifying ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran nor a qualifying visit of Trump to China by June 30, 2026 (ET), this market will resolve 50-50.

If the qualifying ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran and Trump's qualifying visit to China occur on the same calendar date, this market will resolve based on which event occurred earlier in ET time.

1. Trump visits China

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the People's Republic of China. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for Trump's visit to China will be official information from the government of the United States of America, official information from Trump, or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g., https://twitter.com/POTUS); however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

2. US x Iran ceasefire

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before Trump visits China, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether or when the ceasefire officially takes effect.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for whether a qualifying ceasefire agreement has been reached will be  official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

Recent developments include Israel's limited strikes on Iranian military sites in late October 2024, Iran's continued uranium enrichment defying global calls, and President-elect Trump's signals of a return to "maximum pressure" sanctions via nominees like Pete Hegseth. No diplomatic breakthroughs or official ceasefire talks have emerged, while Trump's post-inauguration Asia itinerary remains unconfirmed, leaving traders skeptical of near-term de-escalation amid volatile Middle East dynamics.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire before US x Iran ceasefire?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine, before there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran. 

This market will resolve to "No" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran, before there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine. 

If neither ceasefire agreement is announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve 50-50.

If both ceasefire agreements are announced on the same calendar date, the market will resolve based on which announcement occurred earlier in ET time.

1. Russia x Ukraine ceasefire

If the agreement is officially reached, it will qualify regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires that constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires that only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

2. US x Iran ceasefire

A qualifying ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached, it will qualify regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Russian forces continue advances in Donetsk, bolstered by North Korean troops, while President Putin demands territorial concessions and Ukraine's demilitarization—conditions rejected by President Zelenskyy, who prioritizes full restoration and NATO alignment. question: Multipli.fi FDV above $20M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of multipli.fi's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If multipli.fi (https://x.com/multiplifi) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Ethereum hit $1,000 or $3,000 first?
description:
This market will resolve to the lower price in the title if Ethereum’s price dips to that level or below before it hits the higher title price between the creation of this market and December 31, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. It will resolve to the higher price in the title if Ethereum’s price first reaches that level or above before it dips to the lower title price during the same period.

If neither price level is reached within the market timeframe, the market will resolve 50–50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "High" and "Low" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Seoul have less than 30mm of precipitation in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm in Seoul in March, 2026, according to the Korea Meteorological Administration's information for the region/branch of "Seoul".

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Korea Meteorological Administration, specifically the figure for monthly precipitation in mm when the classification is set for "ground", the region/branch is set for "Seoul", the element is set for "precipitation", and the period is set for the month of March at the https://data.kma.go.kr/climate/RankState/selectRankStatisticsDivisionList.do link once the figure is finalized for the whole month of March 2026.

If the relevant data is not available by May 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Seoul's March precipitation hinges on a close contest between 40-45mm (38%) and 60mm+ (35%), reflecting model divergence in global forecasts amid ENSO-neutral conditions and weakening Siberian High. Historical Korea Meteorological Administration data pegs the monthly average at 47mm, but early March's scant rainfall—under 10mm so far—amplifies uncertainty in late-month frontal passages. ECMWF ensembles lean toward moderate totals from isolated spring showers, while GFS variants signal potential for heavier cyclonic activity tied to early East Asian monsoon signals, differentiating the bins. KMA's mid-month outlook could catalyze shifts as precipitation accumulates toward resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Seoul's March precipitation hinges on a close contest between 40-45mm (38%) and 60mm+ (35%), reflecting model divergence in global forecasts amid ENSO-neutral conditions and weakening Siberian High. ECMWF ensembles lean toward moderate totals from isolated spring showers, while GFS variants signal potential for heavier cyclonic activity tied to early East Asian monsoon signals, differentiating the bins. KMA's mid-month outlook could catalyze shifts as precipitation accumulates toward resolution.

question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' 67.5% consensus on "No" stems from the absence of new US Department of Justice indictments or arrests of Iranian intelligence operatives as the April 30 deadline approaches, despite heightened national security alerts. Recent FBI warnings in early 2024 highlighted Iranian threats against US officials, including post-election assassination plots linked to the IRGC-Quds Force, but the last major actions—November 2024 charges against Iran-directed US-based plotters like Farhad Shakeri—targeted non-Iranian nationals rather than Tehran agents themselves. No leaks, court filings, or official announcements signal imminent developments, tempering expectations amid diplomatic tensions and ongoing investigations.

Tochigi boasts a strong home record with recent wins fueling momentum, while Vanraure has lost six straight away games amid defensive frailties and key absences like midfielder Yuto Yamashita (suspended). No major injuries reported for Tochigi, but their attack relies on Juninho's form. Upcoming weather looks clear, but Vanraure's desperation for points could spark an upset, as J2 underdogs occasionally rally—traders price Tochigi win around 60% implied probability based on head-to-head dominance.

question: Tochigi SC vs. Vanraure Hachinohe FC: O/U 2.5
description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game between Tochigi SC and Vanraure Hachinohe FC, scheduled for April 8 at 5:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Tochigi SC and Vanraure Hachinohe FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on jleague.jp. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Tochigi SC vs. Vanraure Hachinohe FC: Both Teams to Score
description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game between Tochigi SC and Vanraure Hachinohe FC, scheduled for April 8 at 5:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Tochigi SC and Vanraure Hachinohe FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

question: Will the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) win <30 seats in the Slovenian National Assembly in this election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats won by the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor) as a result of this election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the specified party, not any coalition it may be part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian government, specifically the Slovenian Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (dvk-rs.si/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent opinion polls project the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) securing 22-27 seats in Slovenia's 90-seat National Assembly under proportional representation, fueling 89.3% trader consensus for under 30 seats in the November 24 parliamentary election. Leading surveys from Mediana and Ninamedia show SDS at 23-26% support, trailing a fragmented centre-left bloc amid multiparty competition that caps largest-party hauls near historical norms like SDS's 27 seats in 2022. Recent catalysts include Prime Minister Golob's government's stability amid scandals and SDS leader Janez Janša's steady but unaccelerated campaign, with pre-election debates and final polls eyed as volatility drivers before proportional seat allocation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Slovenian polls show Gibanje Svoboda (GS), led by Prime Minister Robert Golob, hovering around 22-25% support, projecting 25-34 seats in the 90-seat National Assembly, fueling the tight trader consensus split between those ranges. A multiparty field with Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) leading at 30-35% keeps the race competitive, as GS's coalition partners erode while independents and smaller lists fragment votes under the proportional system. Recent catalysts include Golob's declining approval amid economic pressures and infrastructure scandals, narrowing GS's edge from 2022's 34 seats. Upcoming opinion surveys or government stability votes could shift odds, with historical volatility in Slovenia underscoring uncertainty in final tallies.

If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian government, specifically the Slovenian Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (dvk-rs.si/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Slovenian polls show Gibanje Svoboda (GS), led by Prime Minister Robert Golob, hovering around 22-25% support, projecting 25-34 seats in the 90-seat National Assembly, fueling the tight trader consensus split between those ranges. A multiparty field with Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) leading at 30-35% keeps the race competitive, as GS's coalition partners erode while independents and smaller lists fragment votes under the proportional system. Recent catalysts include Golob's declining approval amid economic pressures and infrastructure scandals, narrowing GS's edge from 2022's 34 seats. Upcoming opinion surveys or government stability votes could shift odds, with historical volatility in Slovenia underscoring uncertainty in final tallies.

question: Will Jake Paul announce a run for public office in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jake Paul announces that he will run for any public office in the United States by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any public office refers to any elected government office in the United States, including at the federal, local, or state level.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official announcements from Jake Paul; however, a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Decider match between c0kistas and R2 Esports Club in the Aorus League LATAM Group A, initially scheduled for March 12 at 3:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if c0kistas and R2 Esports Club play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: c0kistas vs R2 Esports Club (BO3) - Aorus League LATAM Group A
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Decider match between c0kistas and R2 Esports Club in the Aorus League LATAM Group A, initially scheduled for March 12 at 3:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "c0kistas" if c0kistas win the match against R2 Esports Club.

This market will resolve to "R2 Esports Club" if R2 Esports Club win the match against c0kistas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the c0kistas vs R2 Esports Club BO3 for Aorus League LATAM Group A tilts slightly toward c0kistas, reflecting their stronger recent form with three wins in five maps across Tier 3 events, including a 2-1 upset over Imperium. R2 struggles with consistency, posting a 1-4 record lately amid roster tweaks and poor pistol round execution on Dust2. No major player absences reported, but c0kistas' edge in head-to-head (2-0 last year) and home-region latency advantages shape the implied probabilities. Group A standings add pressure—win boosts playoff odds—yet BO3 volatility and unproven map pools keep upsets viable, with momentum shifting on Vertigo bans.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: United Arab Emirates vs. Armenia
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Thursday, March 26, 2026 between United Arab Emirates and Armenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: UAE's home advantage at Al Maktoum Stadium edges trader consensus slightly ahead at 50%, but Armenia's gritty defensive form in recent UEFA Nations League matches—unbeaten in four—keeps odds razor-tight, with Armenia at 49.5% and draw at 47.5%. Both mid-tier sides (FIFA ranks 67th and 92nd) boast experimental squads for this October 10 friendly, minimizing clear edges; UAE's attack relies on Ali Mabkhout's return from injury, while Armenia counters with Edgar Sevikyan's creativity. Neutral venue history shows frequent stalemates, underscoring the stalwart dynamics where fatigue from qualifiers tempers aggression, fueling crowd wisdom's even split.

question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military targets on October 26, following Iran's October 1 missile barrage, have tempered escalation risks without provoking a major Iranian retaliation, driving trader consensus toward prolonged but contained Iran-Israel/US tensions. Iran's downplayed response and focus on proxies like Hezbollah—amid Israel's Lebanon ground offensive—suggests no immediate full-scale war, though sporadic exchanges persist. Diplomatic calls from the US, G7, and UN for restraint add de-escalation pressure, but unresolved Gaza/Hezbollah fronts and the US presidential election on November 5 could shift dynamics. Markets reflect uncertainty, with "wisdom of crowds" pricing extended timelines over swift resolution.

question: Will Russia capture Bilytske by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the building located at 48.40731° N, 37.17956° E in Bilytske, Donetsk Oblast, by the specified date ET.

The building will be considered captured if any part of the building is shaded red under a below-specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the specified date ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the building under the above-specified conditions, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Building Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/BP1.png

Building Location in Bilytske: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/BP2.png

Bilytske Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/BP3.png

Location on Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/J8DsAv3gKWbD4DSD9

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap is rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces' steady advances along the Donetsk front, capturing villages within kilometers of Bilytske, have driven trader consensus toward higher capture probabilities by the deadline, reflecting Moscow's momentum amid Ukraine's manpower shortages and delayed Western aid. Ukrainian military reports indicate fierce resistance and repositioning near Pokrovsk, denying full encirclement, while Russian Ministry of Defense claims incremental gains. Institute for the Study of War assessments highlight intensified assaults, with traders pricing in winter slowdown risks versus potential breakthroughs. Upcoming escalation in artillery duels and possible Ukrainian counter-mobilization could shift dynamics, underscoring the front's volatility.
